# [clang] Assertion `(!Is64Bit || isInt<32>((long long)FIOffset + Imm)) && "Requesting 64-bit offset in 32-bit immediate!"' failed

**Author:** ym-xie
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/121932
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** backend:X86, llvm:codegen, crash, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2025-08-11T15:53:47Z

## Body

code:
```c
static int i = 0;
int main(void) {
  char s[] = "t";
  extern int putchar(int);
  putchar(s[-2147483648]);
  return 0;
}
```
just crash on trunk with assertion enabled.
https://godbolt.org/z/YPhYYYTjz
Generated by fuzzing
I didn't find any similar issues.

<details>
code:
```c
static int i = 24;
int main(void) {
  char s[] = "tuesday";
  extern int putchar(int);
  while (i &gt; 0) {
    putchar(s[-2147483648]);
  }
  return 0;
}
```
crash clang
https://godbolt.org/z/G177j5hYa
Generated by fuzzing

### Comment 2 - ym-xie

code
```c
int main(void) {
    char x[2147483647];
    x[-42] = '!';
}
```
https://godbolt.org/z/5Ynr6fccE
above code als crash

---

### Comment 3 - abhishek-kaushik22

Looking at the history of this assertion, it used to be 
```
  int64_t Offset = FIOffset + Imm;
  if (!Is64Bit) {
    assert(isInt<32>((long long)FIOffset + Imm) &&
           "Requesting 64-bit offset in 32-bit immediate!");
  }
```
before it was changed to
```
assert((!Is64Bit || isInt<32>((long long)FIOffset + Imm)) &&
           "Requesting 64-bit offset in 32-bit immediate!");
```

Shouldn't it be `assert(!Is64Bit && isInt<32>((long long)FIOffset + Imm))`?

---

### Comment 4 - RKSimon

Shouldn't it be:
```c
assert((Is64Bit || isInt<32>((long long)FIOffset + Imm)) &&
           "Requesting 64-bit offset in 32-bit immediate!");
```
We only want the assert testing isInt<> in the !Is64Bit case.

---

### Comment 5 - RKSimon

It appears we have 2 asserts in X86RegisterInfo.cpp which do the same thing

---

### Comment 6 - RKSimon

Actually maybe not: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/commit/bc2ae1c8

---
